observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require:
a. The Municipal Treasurer to inform the concerned payees that their
checks have become stale and needed to be cancelled or replaced upon
presentation of the said staled checks and report to the Municipal
Accounting Office the stale checks for proper adjustment in the books
of accounts; and
b. Both the Municipal Treasurer and OIC-Municipal Accountant to
reconcile their Cash in Bank balances with their cashbook and ledger,
respectively.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. OIC-Municipal Accountant to consider the conditions stated in
Paragraphs 67 and 68 of IPSAS 29 in the recognition of impairment
loss and provision of allowance for impairment; and
b. Municipal Treasurer and the focal persons of the loan programs to
develop appropriate collection strategies to recover the past due loan
receivables from other debtors/borrowers to ensure cash availability,
maintain continuity of the livelihood program, and provide equal
opportunity for other prospective beneficiaries in availing capital for
livelihood programs.

a. Municipal Treasurer to (i) prioritize the updating of the RPTAR/TIC
to facilitate setting up of correct RPT/SET Receivables account balance
at the beginning of the year and to reconcile with the records of the
Municipal Assessor’s Office and; (ii) develop a more aggressive and
effective strategy in tax administration that will intensify collections.
Exert more effort to enforce collection and develop strategy procedure/
system to improve the collections efficiency and collection from
delinquent taxpayers; and
b. Municipal Assessor to synchronize assessment records with the
collection records of individual taxpayers of the Municipal Treasurer’s
Office (MTO).

a. Municipal Treasurer to prepare and maintain complete and updated
perpetual inventory records which should always reconcile with the
records of the Accounting Office; and
b. Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical count of all
Inventories of the Municipality, prepare and submit the corresponding
RPCI in the prescribed format and with complete details, and submit
the same to the Office of the Auditor not later than July 31 and January
31 of each year.

a. Municipal Treasurer to prepare and maintain complete and updated
perpetual inventory records which should always reconcile with the
records of the Accounting Department pursuant to the provisions of
Section 119 of Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I; and
b. Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical count of all PPEs
of the Municipality, ensure that the RPCPPE is in the prescribed
format and with complete details, and invite the COA Audit Team to
witness the physical inventory taking.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal
Agriculturist to submit the FURs to the DA to account for the partial liquidation
of fund transfer and eventual reconciliation with their books of accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, thru the Finance Committee:
(a) strategize to increase its collection efficiency and exercise prudence in handling
government funds pursuant to Section 2 of PD No. 1445; (b) consider the
availability of funds before incurring obligations to maintain its ability to meet
current maturing obligations; and (c) develop a sound internal control on cash
and ensure that its spending will not exceed its collection/income.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal
Development Council (MDC):
a. Reprogram the idle PPAs under the Continuing and Reprogrammed
Appropriations to another developmental PPAs that are relevant to the
current times and needs of the Municipality and its constituents; and
b. Ensure the optimal utilization of the 20% DF, including the Continuing
and Reprogrammed Appropriations, through timely implementation
of the PPAs to maximize resource allocation and fulfill the objectives of
the fund, ultimately benefiting the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. BAC to strictly comply with the provisions of the updated 2016 Revised
IRR of RA No. 9184 regarding the completeness and propriety of
procurement documents, invitation to observers, and posting
requirements, among others, to enhance transparency and establish the
regularity of the procurement process;
b. OIC-Municipal Accountant to check the completeness and propriety of
supporting documents prescribed under Sections 9.1 and 9.2 of COA
Circular No. 2012-001 before processing claims for payments to ensure
compliance with the updated 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and to
establish the legality, validity, and propriety of the disbursements; and
c. Concerned offices (BAC, Accounting and Engineering) to submit the
required supporting documents as discussed in the preceding
paragraphs in accordance with the updated 2016 Revised IRR of RA
No. 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 to enable to arrive at a
decision in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Engineer to direct the concerned contractor to correct/rectify the deficiencies
noted in the afore-mentioned infrastructure projects and submit to the Audit
Team the compliance report together with the pictures before, during and after
rectification of deficiencies, otherwise, require payment of the corresponding costs
of the defects from the contractor.

OIC-Municipal Accountant to (i) make the necessary adjustment on the fund transfer amounting to P38,074.97, identified in the SL as “Assistance from National Government”, to cleanse the Due to NGAS account; and (ii) reclassify the financial assistance from the NDRRMC from the Due to NGAs account to the Trust Liability-DRRM account for fair and correct presentation of the account balances in the financial statements; and

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, being the Chairperson of the Local DRRM Council, cause for the optimal utilization of the LDRRMF to strengthen the resilience of the constituents to disasters, climate change impacts and the threats brought about by the COVID-19 pandemic.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor effectively cause the optimal utilization of the 20percent Development Fund to spur development, provide jobs to the local constituents and to achieve the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, thru the Municipal Engineering Office and Municipal Planning and Development Office, ensure the optimal utilization of the 20% Development Fund to spur development, provide jobs to the local constituents and to achieve the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The utilization rate under the 20 percent Development increased from 26.10 percent in CY 2019 to 52% in CY 2020.
observations.reason: Some projects were not implemented due to the outbreak of COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We therefore recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Accountant, enforce the settlement of audit suspensions and disallowances by requiring the persons responsible, therefore, within the prescribed period as provided under Sections 7.1, 9.4 and 10.4, of the RRSA prescribed under COA Circular No. 2009-006.
observations.managementAction: Settlements totaling P839,248.68 were made by the concerned persons liable, equivalent to 78.46 percent of the total Notices of Disallowance at the beginning of period.
observations.reason: No settlement was made on the Notices of Suspension because of oversight of the concerned persons responsible.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the a) Inventory Committee, if any, to conduct the physical count of all Inventory and PPE and submit the RPCI and RPCPPE to the Audit Team as prescribed under Section 124 of the NGAS Manual, copy furnish the Municipal Accountant to facilitate reconciliation with accounting records; b) Municipal Treasurer/General Services Officer to maintain complete and updated perpetual inventory records which should always reconcile with the PPE general ledger balances.  Any discrepancy between these records should be properly examined to facilitate adjustment of the affected PPE account balances for its fair and accurate presentation in the Statement of Financial Position.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Accountant, (a) send demand letter to concerned AO’s to enforce liquidation of cash advance; (b) strictly monitor the grant and liquidation of cash advances to ensure that these are liquidated within the prescribed period;                              (c) prohibit/refrain from granting additional cash advances to those employees who still have unliquidated balances; (d) ensure that all cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of the year in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002; and (e) reconcile the financial statement account balance of P249,981.35 with the RUCA to ensure correctness.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and other concerned departments to (i) assess the collection efficiency, (ii) take possible steps/strategies to collect the loan receivable from borrowers to ensure cash availability for future beneficiaries of various loan programs, and (iii) exert more efforts to collect those past due accounts. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor evaluate the effectiveness of the program to serve as guide in determining whether to continue the program or to suspend until all the receivables are collected.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause the transfer of the remaining PNB account balances to any of the authorized government depository banks in the nearby Municipality of Mamburao or secure approval and/or clearance from the DOF authorizing PNB as the duly approved depository bank, if the Municipality still intends to continue with PNB’s services.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the validity of the unliquidated obligations or accounts payable which were outstanding for more than two (2) years to five (5) years and cause the reversion of these accounts to the unappropriated surplus, if warranted.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
Municipal Engineer to implement promptly the projects funded out of grants from various NGAs in accordance with the Memorandum of Agreement; and
The Municipal Accountant to evaluate the need for those unexpended fund balances from NGAs. If still needed, Management should request for authority from the source agency to use the money for other related projects of the Municipality, otherwise, it should be returned to the source agencies as required under existing rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
Bids and Awards Committee to (i) strictly comply with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 on the procurement of goods through public bidding and other modes of procurement and ensure strict adherence of the conditions/requirements under Annex H Consolidated Guidelines for the Alternative Methods of Procurement; and (ii) demand from the Supplier the posting of warranty security in order to fully protect the interest of the Municipality;
Municipal Accountant to review the regularity and completeness of supporting documents prescribed under Section 9.1 and 9.2.5 of COA Circular No. 2012 – 001 before processing claims for payment and submit the duly accomplished JEV; and
BAC/GSO to observe the timely submission of advance copies of contracts and its supporting documents within five (5) working days after its perfection pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to (a) use 5% VAT in computing the ABC pursuant to the DPWH Department Order No. 22 as amended by DPWH DO No. 197 series of 2016;     (b) closely monitor/supervise project implementation to ensure contractor’s compliance with the plan and technical specifications of the contract; and (c) compute the liquidated damages on project with delayed completion.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) to (a) maximize the utilization of the 70% LDRRMF for the programs, projects and activities identified for disaster preparedness, prevention and mitigation in order to strengthen the resilience of the constituents to calamities and climate change impacts; and (b) procure the basic equipment and other equipage needed to effectively respond to imminent or current hazard events or conditions and ensure public safety towards meeting the basic needs of the constituents in case of catastrophes.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer (a) enforce the settlement of suspensions and disallowances by requiring, the persons responsible and/or liable to settle their accountabilities within the prescribed period as provided under Section 9.4 and 10.4 of the COA Circular No. 2009-006; and (b) submit the official receipts and/or other necessary documents as proof of payments/settlement of disallowances to enable the issuance of NSSDC.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee who will conduct physical count of all Inventory and PPE and submit the RPCI and RPCPPE to the Office of the Auditor on time as prescribed under Section 124 of the NGAS Manual, copy furnish the Municipal Accountant to facilitate reconciliation with accounting records.  The Municipal Treasurer/ General Services Officer was advised to maintain complete and updated perpetual inventory records which should always reconcile with the PPE general ledger balances.  Any discrepancy between these records should be properly examined to facilitate adjustment of the affected PPE account balances for its fair presentation in the Statement of Financial Position.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive (LCE) directed all department heads to have the list of equipment entrusted to all officials and employees.
observations.reason: It takes time for the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records because the RPCI and RPCPPE were not completed and submitted by the Inventory Committee promptly. The RPCPPE and RPCI for CY 2018 were submitted only on June 03, 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management, through the Municipal Accountant,        (a) send letter of demand to concerned AO’s to enforce liquidation of cash advance; (b) strictly monitor the grant and liquidation of cash advances to ensure that these are liquidated within the prescribed period; (c) stop granting additional cash advances to those employees who still have unliquidated balance; (d) ensure that all cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of the year in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002; and (e) reconcile the account balance of P571,781.10 and the RUCA to ensure its correctness.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant did not allow the grant of additional cash advance to those employees with unliquidated balances and required the employees to refund the excess on their prior year’s cash advances.
observations.reason: Cash advances were still not liquidated on time due to failure of the Municipal Accountant to monitor the prescribed period for the settlement of their cash advances. The cash advance of the former storekeeper amounting to P82,406.01 which aged more than 5 years was not reduced/refunded however, the release of his benefits such as terminal leave was put on hold and they agreed to pledge his benefits to settle his long outstanding cash advances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the HRMO and Accountant strictly adhere to the Rules and Regulations of the Civil Service Commission in processing application for monetization of leave credits of local employees. Also, ensure the completeness of supporting documents before processing claims for payment to establish the regularity and validity of the disbursements.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer observe the proper classification of expenses from budgeting to recording of expenses to arrive at the correct balance of the affected accounts and to avoid misstatements in the financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management, through the person in charge of the payroll to use the maximum amount of compensation which shall not be more than P5,000.00 as basis in computing the 2% employee and employer’s share on Pag-IBIG premium in compliance with Section 7 of RA No. 9679.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer (MSWDO) or its representative to (i) monitor the Core Shelter Assistance Program/Project and fast track the implementation/construction of the core shelter units to comply with the timeframe stated in the Memorandum of Agreement; (ii) to settle the issues on the revision of MOA between the Municipality and DSWD by coordinating with the said agency; (iii) instruct the officers of the NASA to deposit all the amount released by the LGU to the members/beneficiaries to their depository account to avoid misuse and possible loss; and (iv) ensure that the documents in the procurement of materials used in the implementation of CSAP are secured to facilitate prompt liquidation of the financial assistance with the DSWD.
observations.managementAction: The target housing unit for the 1st tranche of CSAP CY 2017  were completed and submitted the Fund Utilization Report to the DSWD. The MSWDO requested original copy of the revised MOA from the DSWD and submit the same to the Audit Team. The 2nd tranche of CSAP from the DSWD amounting to P16,643,658.00 was received in May 2018. The amount is still with the Land Bank of the Philippines and not yet distributed to NASA since the MSWD and CSAP focal person are still coordinating with the NASA to assist them in registering their association with the SEC. This is necessary in order to deposit their fund with authorized government depository bank under the name of their association.

The management procured only those drugs and medicines included in the Essential Drug List of the latest PNDF/PNF Manual, Current Edition. Municipal Treasurer/General Services Office accepted first the delivered items first before the same be subjected to inspection by the Inspectorate team. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant accomplished the JEV properly.

Management revised their APP, all procurements made by the BAC for CY 2018 were included in the Annual Procurement Plan. 
BAC complied with the provisions of 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 with regard to the conduct of public bidding on amounts exceeding P50,000.00 and resorted only to other alternative mode after the two (2) failed bidding.
observations.reason: Procedural lapses still occurred in the procurement process hence, lacking some supporting documents were still not compliant. Due to bulk of transactions in the Accounting office they failed to ensure the completeness of the supporting documents.

observations.reason: Procedural lapses still occurred in the procurement process hence, lacking some supporting documents were still not compliant. Due to bulk of transactions in the Accounting office they failed to ensure the completeness of the supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the   (a) Municipal Engineer compute the Approved Budget for the Contract pursuant to the DPWH Department Order No. 22, closely monitor/supervise project implementation to ensure contractor’s compliance with the specifications of the contract and to require the contractor to submit the Test Results, if any, and the As-Built Plan for the project Nos. 1 and 2; (b) Municipal Accountant require the contractor to submit the warranty security for the two project to ensure that the interest of the Municipality is protected against structural defects and failures that may arise within the warranty period; and (c) Municipal Treasurer bill the MGS Builders and General Contractor and require the payment of liquidated damages amounting P82,534.62 for the delays incurred in the implementation of Construction of Level 2 Water Supply System at Sitio Bagong, Silang Brgy. observations.reason: The Municipal Engineer still used the 12% VAT component instead of 5% hence, this finding is reiterated in Part II of the report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

Municipal Planning and Development Officer carry out faithfully the developmental objectives for which 20% Development Fund was created; and

MPDO and the Municipal Engineer conduct regular monitoring on the planned investment projects and evaluate the implementation of the projects to avoid delays and maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund in accordance with DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.
observations.managementAction: MPDO and Municipal Engineer monitored project implementation. CY 2017 financial delivery rate of 30.43% was increased to 37.10% this CY 2018.
observations.reason: Lack of contractors to participate in the bidding resulted to failed biddings thus,  prolonged the procurement process. Road right of way issues,  project location which are in forest land and its accessibility also contributed in delayed project implementation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

Require the MDRRM Officer to monitor compliance with the Disaster Preparedness Minimum Standards, Volume II, series of 2015, issued by the DILG and NDRRMC as regards training, equipages among others, and the full implementation of LDRRM Plan towards the achievement of the objectives of RA No. 10121;

Maximize utilization of the LDRRMF for the programs, projects and activities identified for disaster preparedness, prevention and mitigation in order to strengthen the resilience of the constituents to calamities and climate change impacts.

CY 2017 utilization rate of 22.93% was increased to 68.74% this CY 2018.
observations.reason: Optimal utilization of Mitigation fund for emergency preparedness was not achieved due to lack of available supplier to participate in the bidding.

 require the concerned employees to prepare and submit the GAD Accomplishment Report and ensure that it is accompanied by the documentary requirements provided in Joint Memorandum Circular 2013-01.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management create an Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of all Inventory and PPE and submit the RPCI and RPCPPE to the Office of the Auditor on time as prescribed by Section 124 of NGAS Manual, copy furnish the Municipal Accountant to facilitate reconciliation with accounting records.  The Municipal Treasurer/General Services Officer was advised to maintain complete and updated perpetual inventory records which should always reconcile with the general ledger.  Any discrepancy between these records should be properly examined to facilitate adjustment of the affected Inventory and PPE account balances for its fair presentation in the balance sheet.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant recognize Infrastructure Asset in the books of accounts in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015 to ensure the fair presentation of the account in the Financial Statement.

We recommended that Management, through the Municipal Accountant, strictly monitor the grant and liquidation of cash advances to ensure that cash advances are liquidated within the prescribed period and that all cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of the year in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting entries in order to arrive at the correct amount of Advances to Officers and Employees, Advances for Operating Expenses and Due from Officers and Employees, for their fair presentation in the financial statement.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to observe the proper classification of expenses from budgeting up to their recording in the books of accounts to assure the correctness of informations in the financial statements. Also, the Municipal Budget Officer was advised to include all PS items in the preparation of the annual PS budget and avoid classifying them as MOOE.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause the transfer of depository accounts with PNB to the authorized government depository banks in the nearby Municipality of Mamburao or secure approval and/or clearance from the DOF authorizing PNB as the duly approved depository bank, if the Municipality still intends to continue PNB’s services.

We recommended that the concerned personnel strictly observe the CSC Rules and Regulations in processing application of monetization of leave credits of local employees. Also, ensure the completeness of supporting documents before processing claims for payment to establish the regularity and validity of the related disbursements.

We recommended that the Mayor instruct the personnel in-charge of payroll preparation and other involved units to ensure that the P3,500.00 minimum net take home pay of employees is followed as provided under Section 52 of RA No. 10717.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor submit official receipt from the LMP in order to support the payment of annual fee, pursuant to Section 69 of GAAM, Volume 1.
We recommended for the strict adherence with the New Constitution and By- Laws of the Liga ng mga Baranggay sa Pilipinas with regard to succession of ABC President.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant remit intact the amount Due to BIR and adhere to the provisions of Section 2.81, 4.114 and 2.58(a)(2) of Revenue Regulations 2-98, as amended by Section 5 of Revenue Regulations 17-2003. Also, stop the practice of preparing checks unless the services/goods are received by/delivered to the Municipality.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile monthly the amount withheld and the amount remitted to determine the correctness of the Due to BIR account balance of P2,599,310.86. Prepare the schedule of Due to BIR account and make sure that ledgers are properly accomplished in full detail.

We recommended that the Management insure with the Property Insurance Fund of the GSIS all the insurable properties of the Municipality the soonest time possible to guarantee its indemnification in case of losses/damages due to manmade and catastrophic events.

We recommended that the Management enforce the preparation of PPMPs by end-users for consolidation into an APP by the BAC Secretariat. See to it that the APP is consistent with the Annual Budget, reflects the entire procurement activity of the agency for the calendar year and have all the necessary information required under GPPB Circular 1-2009 dated 20 January 2009.

We recommended that the (a) Bids and Awards Committee strictly comply with the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its Revised IRR; and (b) the Municipal Accountant review and check the completeness of documentary requirements as prescribed under Section 9 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We also recommended that the Management submit certification from the Municipal Health Officer/requisitioning officer that the procured drugs and medicines are in conformity with PNDF Volume I, pursuant to Executive Order No. 49.

We recommended the following:
The approving authorities such as Municipal Mayor, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant ensure completeness of supporting documents prior  to approval/payment of any claim;
The Bids and Awards Committee strictly comply with the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its Revised IRR and to submit the complete documentation prescribed under Section 9 of COA Circular No.        2012 – 001.
The Municipal Engineer strictly comply with the provision cited under DPWH Department Order No. 22 series of 2015 in the preparation of ABC for Infrastructure projects.

We recommended that the Management consolidate purchase requisitions for one or more items needed at or about the same time by the requisitioner and to strictly comply with the provisions of IRR of RA No. 9184 regarding the conduct of public bidding.

We recommended that the Management carry out faithfully the developmental objectives for which 20% Development Fund was created. We also recommend that the Management conduct a regular monitoring on the planned investment projects and its implementation.
We recommended that the Management conduct competitive bidding pursuant to Section 10 of RA No. 9184. Alternative methods of procurement shall be resorted to only in highly exceptional cases provided in the IRR.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and LDRRMO prepare the Monthly Report on Utilization of the DRRM fund for submission to COA on or before the 15th of each month as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2012-002. We also recommended for the full utilization of LDRRMF for the purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Mayor:
 Require the GAD Focal Person to synchronize the  preparation of GPB with the budgeting calendar to enable its timely submission to the PPDO to ensure its alignment with the priorities of the Province and to the DILG for review and approval;
 Ensure that programs, projects and activities charged against GAD Funds are gender-responsive; and
 Ensure that GAD Accomplishment Report is accompanied by the requirements provided in Joint Memorandum Circular 2013-01.
We recommended that Management remit the remaining PDAF balance of P299,975.00 to the Bureau of Treasury in compliance with the Supreme Court decision.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the other concerned persons responsible/liable to settle immediately the suspensions and disallowances. Pursuant to Section 7 of the Rules and Regulation on Settlement of Accounts, the head of the agency is responsible for ensuring that the requirements of transactions suspended in audit are complied with and the settlement of disallowances is made within the prescribed period.

items/service not yet
delivered/rendered.
